interactive_only.description: Only annotate actionable elements (default true; false = every node).

expression.description: JS expression. Wrapped to support top-level await.

since_ms.description: Filter to requests started after this unix-ms timestamp.

selector.description: Optional CSS selector for target. Defaults to document.activeElement, then document.body.

action.description: What to do once the element is located.

nth.description: Index when the selector matches multiple elements (0=first).

selector.description: CSS selector. Use ' >>> ' to pierce open shadow roots (e.g. 'my-host >>> button.submit').

tab_id.description: Target tab id. Defaults to active tab.

value.description: Text for action=fill, or attribute name for action=get_attr.

all_frames.description: Walk every iframe in the tab and merge results (each row carries a frame_id field).

visible_only.description: Filter to elements with non-zero bounding box.

tab_id.description: 🎯 TARGET ANY TAB: Click elements in background tabs without switching! Get tab IDs from tab_list to interact with multiple tabs efficiently.

force_focus.description: Use enhanced focus sequence with click simulation for modern apps

tab_id.description: 🎯 TARGET ANY TAB: Fill forms in background tabs without switching! Perfect for batch form filling across multiple tabs. Get tab IDs from tab_list.

args.description: Arguments forwarded as the function's parameters (JSON-serializable).

expression.description: JS expression OR full function body. The code is wrapped in `() => { <expression> }`, so a trailing `return` is needed for non-expression bodies.

tab_id.description: Target tab id. If omitted, uses active tab.

world.description: Execution world. MAIN sees page globals; ISOLATED is the content-script sandbox.

end_date.description: End date for history search (ISO 8601 format)

keywords.description: Search keywords to match in page titles and URLs

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

sort_by.description: Sort results by visit time, visit count, or title

start_date.description: Start date for history search (ISO 8601 format)

include_metadata.description: Include metadata about the selection (element info, position, etc.)

max_length.description: Maximum length of text to return

tab_id.description: 🎯 TARGET ANY TAB: Get selected text from background tabs without switching! Perfect for collecting quotes or snippets from multiple research tabs.

element_ids.description: Expand specific quick match IDs from discover phase (e.g. ['q1', 'q2'])

focus_areas.description: Areas to analyze in detail: buttons, forms, navigation, search_elements

intent_hint.description: User intent: login, signup, search, post_create, comment, menu, submit, etc.

max_results.description: Maximum number of elements to return

phase.description: Analysis phase: 'discover' for quick scan (<100 tokens), 'detailed' for full analysis

tab_id.description: 🎯 TARGET ANY TAB: Specify tab ID to analyze background tabs without switching! Get tab IDs from tab_list. If omitted, analyzes current active tab.

max_items.description: Maximum number of items to extract (for lists/collections)

summarize.description: Return summary instead of full content to save tokens

tab_id.description: 🎯 TARGET ANY TAB: Extract content from specific background tab without switching! Use tab_list to get tab IDs. Perfect for processing multiple research tabs.

wait_for.description: CSS selector to wait for after navigation (ensures page is ready)

tab_id.description: 🎯 TARGET ANY TAB: Scroll content in background tabs without switching! Perfect for navigating long documents or pages in multiple tabs simultaneously.

remember.description: Remember this style for this website

condition_type.description: Type of condition to wait for

text.description: Text to wait for (when condition_type is 'text_present')

tab_id.description: Tab to capture. Defaults to active tab in current window.

interactive_only.description: If true, only nodes with an actionable role/tag/onclick/tabindex are listed.

max_nodes.description: Soft cap on emitted nodes; sets truncated=true if exceeded.

tab_id.description: Optional tab id; defaults to active tab.
Specific tab ID to close (optional, closes current tab if not provided)

tab_ids.description: Array of tab IDs to close multiple tabs

active.description: Whether to activate the last created tab (single tab only)

batch_settings.description: Performance control settings for batch operations

chunk_size.description: Number of tabs to create per batch

delay_between_tabs.description: Delay between individual tabs in milliseconds

count.description: REQUIRED FOR MULTIPLE IDENTICAL TABS: Set this to N to create N copies of the same URL. For '5 Twitter tabs' use count=5 with url='https://x.com'. DO NOT make 5 separate calls!

timeout.description: Maximum wait time per tab in milliseconds

url.description: Single URL to open. Can be used with 'count' to create multiple identical tabs

urls.description: PREFERRED FOR MULTIPLE URLS: Array of URLs to open ALL AT ONCE in a single batch operation. Pass ALL URLs here instead of making multiple calls! Example: ['https://x.com/post1', 'https://x.com/post2', 'https://google.com']

wait_for.description: CSS selector to wait for after tab creation (single tab only)

check_content_script.description: 🔍 ESSENTIAL FOR BACKGROUND TABS: Check which tabs are ready for background operations! Set to true when planning multi-tab workflows to see which tabs can be targeted.

current_window_only.description: Only return tabs from the current window

include_details.description: Include additional tab details (title, favicon, etc.)

since_ms.description: Only consider downloads started after this unix-ms timestamp.
